content.problemStatement:
Chef has XXX ones (111s) and YYY twos (222s) in his collection. He wants to arrange all of them into the **smallest possible palindrome number†^{\dagger}†** using **all** of these ones (111s) and twos (222s).

Help Chef with the answer.

Note: XXX and YYY are both even numbers.

†^{\dagger}†A **palindromic number** is a number that remains the same when its digits are reversed.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers XXX, and YYY — the amount of ones (111s) and twos (222s) respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **smallest possible palindrome number** using XXX ones (1s) and YYY twos (2s).
constraints: XXX and YYY are both even

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only palindrome number that can be formed using 222 ones (111s) is 111111.
Test case 222: Two possible palindromic numbers can be formed using 222 ones (111s) and 222 twos (222s) which are 122112211221 and 211221122112.
The smaller palindromic number is 122112211221, so that is the answer for this case.
